steps.title: Identify the grammatical chunks
steps.content: On the left of the blank sits the adjective 'Helical', which will combine with the word placed in the blank to form a gerund phrase serving as the sentence’s subject. On the right of the blank begins the participial phrase 'widely understood to confer stability and efficiency…' that modifies that subject.

steps.title: Apply the participial‑phrase rule
steps.content:
A participial phrase that follows the noun it modifies must be set off by commas, so the subject needs a comma after it — choice D provides 'swimming,'.
The other choices either lack that required comma (B and C) or place a comma that wrongly separates the subject from its verb (A).
That matches choice D — D is correct.
